$comment: Versioned schema for config.yaml. `configVersion` is the CURRENT config-shape version the plugin expects; config.yaml carries `config_version`. On every config read a skill compares the two (see references/template.md → Config): equal proceeds, behind offers migration, ahead errors. Bump configVersion when the config shape changes in a way needing migration.
title: Butler config
description: Shape of config.yaml — the only user-specific surface. Project and tag names resolve to ids at runtime; never hard-code ids.

config_version.description: The config-shape version this file was written for. Compared on every read against this schema's `configVersion`. A file with no `config_version` is treated as v0 and offered migration.

planning_project.description: TickTick list where the daily planning ritual lives.

calendar.description: Source of fixed commitments (meetings). Optional — plan runs calendar-blind without it.

source.description: google = read via a connected Google Calendar MCP; none = calendar-blind.

calendars.description: Which calendars to read (names/ids). Default the primary.

pacing.description: Burnout-detection tunables (config_version 2+). All relative to the user's own baseline, never absolute hours. plan fires the no-blame prompt only on the rising edge of sustained overload with no recovery day. See references/heuristics.md → Pacing.

window_days.description: Rolling window (days) for the overload signal.

min_packed_streak.description: Consecutive packed days before the rising edge can fire.

packed_quantile.description: A day is 'packed' if above this quantile of the user's own ~30-day load.

recovery_fraction.description: A 'light/recovery' day is below this fraction of the user's median load. No recovery day in the window is mandatory to fire.

high_completion.description: Framing only, NOT a detection gate: above this the prompt leans 'finishing everything' vs 'falling behind'.

work.description: Work context: pipeline decomposition, packed scheduling. Owns its scheduling config.

breaks.description: Reserved as scheduling constraints (gaps the packer subtracts), NEVER materialized as tasks.
